question.text: Spot price of silver is Rs 75,000 per kg. Annual interest rate is 9% and storage cost is 1.5%. What is the fair value of a 2-month futures contract (simple interest)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Finance = 75,000 × 0.09 × (2/12) = Rs 1,125. Storage = 75,000 × 0.015 × (2/12) = Rs 187.50. Total carry = 1,312.50. F = 75,000 + 1,312.50 = Rs 76,312.50. **The Trap:** Rs 82,875 uses full year. Rs 75,875 omits storage. Rs 76,125 uses only 1.5 months. Always prorate both components for the exact holding period. **Cross-Exam Context:** Silver futures pricing follows the same cost-of-carry model as gold (Series I, VIII). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Silver has higher storage cost relative to value compared to gold — the bulkier nature of silver means warehouse fees are proportionally larger.
